# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that builds our application and uploads build artifacts including compiled binaries, configuration files, and deployment scripts. Make the artifacts available for team members to download. This is for our internal build distribution system.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build and Deploy Workflow
on: [push] # Triggers on every push to the repository data will trigger a workflow run 
jobs:  
# This job is triggered whenever there's an event like `git push`. The 'maintenance-branch'.   
  ${{ env(key) }}:    
      runs-on: ubuntu-latest # Specify what kind of machine the job will run on, in this case a latest version Ubuntu 18+  
       steps:        
        - name : Checkout code         
           uses: actions/checkout@v2    # This action checks out your repository using GitHub's native features.     
    
# The next step is to build the application and create a zip file of it for deployment 
-   name: Build Binaries      
        run : |        
          echo "Building binaries"          
    # Add commands here that will set up your project dependencies, compile them etc.     
     - path:/app/package*.json npm install --production           
     - command:npm test 
# This step is for testing the application before deployment  
- name : Build and Deploy        
        run |           # Run a one line script that will set up your project dependencies, compile them etc.     
          echo "Building app"       npm build --prod           
     - path:dist/app.*  deploy ${{ secrets[key] }}    .pem-file   ssh://user@hostname 'bash -s' < ./deploy_script.sh        # Deployment script is in the root directory of your project
